title: armeria plantaginea subsp. praecox
intro: Armeria plantaginea subsp. praecox, commonly known as the early thimbleweed, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the family Plumbaginaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The early thimbleweed is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving coughs, and soothing digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Armeria plantaginea subsp. praecox include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the plant's leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol or glycerin.
